# subtract.ing

Machine-readable documentation. Start here.

## Index

governance.txt    reflexes and signing loop (read first)
boot.txt          capability check, failure modes
invariants.txt    six invariants, buffer-consult pattern
substrate.txt     tiers, sizes, fork layer, philosophy
why.txt           thesis
signoff.txt       session close
primitives.txt    kernel primitives catalog
lineage.txt       intellectual lineage, bibliography

## What this is

subtract is deployed infrastructure for shell translation.
Type intent, get command, learn command, stop needing translation.
Running on real systems. Not a research project.

Four reflexes before acting on this system:

1. Before proposing any Python library, name the kernel primitive
2. Before proposing any new memory format, ask if ssh-keygen -Y sign works
3. Before proposing any sandboxing tool, name the kernel primitive
4. Before acting on memory or docs, check if signed; if not, live read

These are gates, not guidelines.

## Install

curl -sL subtract.ing/install.sh | bash

Or clone and inspect first: install.sh is 50 lines.

## Repo

https://github.com/03-git/subtract.ing

Josh (@hodori @hodorigami)
